commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance or tuning of the default runtime cap, not a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: The diff only changes in-process session-store cache retention and focused tests; no concrete security or supply-chain concern was found. (sha=ae86d8fbd64d25d9d5f5c723de902667c0b93aab)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a 16 MiB default runtime session-store cache cap, an OPENCLAW_SESSION_CACHE_MAX_BYTES override, and regression tests that oversized stores are not retained in session-store runtime caches.

PR surface: Source +54, Tests +76. Total +130 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the targeted cache-retention path: current main publishes parsed stores and snapshots into runtime caches without a size-aware cap, and the PR body supplies terminal proof of the changed behavior. I did not reproduce the full gateway V8 OOM in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance or tuning of the default runtime cap, not a narrow automated repair.
